content_en:
A: If he had intercourse with her after giving birth, his intention and the reason for such an oath are to be taken into consideration. If he swore this oath: for a certain reason and now this reason is non-existent, no divorce is effected according to the most appropriate opinion held by scholars such as Imam Ahmed and others. If a man swore

When a man says to his wife: “You are thrice divorced” and he has the intention to make it pending, is divorce effected?

Q: A man got angry with his wife and he said to her: “You are thrice divorced”. She said: “now”, and he replied: “now”. He has the intention to make this divorce pending, is it effected?

If a man divorces his wife as a sort of joking, it is effected because he means to divorce her even if he does not intend to put it into effect. The man in question does not mean to divorce his wife and he was not joking as well. This case is just like a man who saw a woman and he said “you are divorced” believing that she is not his wife but it turned to be his wife. In this case also, no divorce is effected according to the most appropriate opinion held by scholars. Allah knows best.

Suraij Question Is the Suraij Question true?

Q: Is Suraij Question true? If it is not, what about a man who imitated him and acted accordingly? When he knew that it is not true, he repented and asked Allah for forgiveness. Does Allah forgive him?

A: All praise be to Allah, the Lord of the Worlds. This question is a sort of innovation in Islam. None of the Prophet’s Companions, their followers or the four Imams adopted it. Some later scholars adopted it, but most Muslim scholars denied it. If a person imitates another as far as this question is concerned and later he repented, Allah will forgive him. He should not divorce his wife even if he married her depending on the interpretation of this question. Allah knows best.

Is it permissible to make a marriage contract in which divorce is stipulated?

Q: A man married a woman and she gave birth to a child. The witnesses advised him that once he consummates marriage with her to say to her: If I divorce you, you are divorced. Is this contract permissible?

a girl and claimed that she belongs to the first husband. Is such claim true? Please be acknowledged that she and the first husband live in the same town and she did not demand for alimony or expenses for the girl.

A: All praise be to Allah. This girl does not belong to the first husband once she claimed so according to the unanimous agreement of Muslim scholars. Even if she gave birth to the girl when she is divorced and she claimed that the girl belongs to him but he denied so, her claim is not accepted unless she has proved it.

According to Abu Hanifah and one of Ahmed’s narraions, a woman is enough as a witness in this regard. According to Malik and the other narration of Ahmed, two women are required. ShafiI stipulates that there should be four women. As for the man, it is enough if he swore an oath denying that the child belongs to him.

If they are still married and live as man and wife, there are two opinions in Ahmed’s Juristic School. The first one is that her claim is not accepted according to Shafi*i’s opinion, while the second is that it is accepted according to Malik’s opinion. If her waiting period is over and most of her pregnancy period passed, and then she claimed that such fetus belongs to the first husband who divorced her, her claim is not accepted at all according to the unanimous agreement of Muslim scholars. If she said that her waiting period is over and she gave birth to a baby after six months of pregnancy or more, does the baby belong to the

There are two opinions held by scholars in this regard. Ahmed, Abu Hanifah, and Ibn Suraij believe that the child belongs to him. Shafi*i and Malik see that the child does not belong to him. This controversial point is applicable if she did not marry. Yet, if she married after her waiting period is over and then gave birth to a baby after more than six months of pregnancy, it does not belong to the first husband according to the unanimous agreement of Muslim scholars.

As the opinions of the four Juristic Schools are clarified, how comes that the girl belongs to the first husband after the lapse of six years? Even if she said that she had given birth to the girl before he divorced her, her claim is not accepted. His claim is accepted that she does not give birth to the girl during the conjugal period, and he should swear an oath on that. If she said that she gave birth to the girl before she got married to the second husband, while the first denied this, his denial is accepted especially that her claim was very late until she married the second husband. This indicates that she is a liar, in particular according to Malik’s juristic school as far as late inexcusable claims are concerned.

A man divorced his wife but a Mufti delivered him a fatwa that divorce is not effected. The man had intercourse with his wife and she gave birth to a child. Was the child born out of adultery?

itself was invalid and the wife gave birth to a baby out of such intercourse, the baby belongs to the husband and they inherit each other. The baby belongs to him because he believes that the marriage is contract is legal and that divorce is not effected because of a wrong fatwa or any other reason. This case is also applicable on invalid contracts according to the unanimous agreement of Muslim scholars. What about the controversial contract?

Even if a man had intercourse with a woman during an invalid marriage such as that made only for pleasure (Mut*ah), or a marriage held without a custodian and witnesses, but he believes it to be legal, the born child belongs to him.

Then what about a controversial marriage although it is proven to be legal according to the Holy Qur’an, the Prophet’s Sunnah and analogy?

When a woman gives birth to a baby just two months after the marriage contract although the

husband had not consummated marriage with her, is such marriage valid?

Q: A woman gave birth to a baby just two months after the marriage contract is made although the husband had not

consummated marriage with her, is such marriage valid? Is he entitled to pay an alimony?

A: All praise be to Allah. According to the unanimous

agreement of Muslims the baby does not belong to him and

he is not entitled to pay a dowry. Yet, scholars hold tow

opinions on the marriage contract:

1.The most appropriate opinion is that it is invalid according to the juristic schools of Malik and Ahmed as well as others. Therefore, marriage must come to an end without paying dowry, as is the case in any other invalid contract. It is rather better if a judge shouldered the responsibility of putting an end to such marriage as a sort of preventing any dispute.

2.The contract is valid but the husband should not consummate marriage until she gives birth. This is the opinion of Abu Hanifah, while Shafi~I believes that he can consummate marriage before giving birth.

If she is pregnant out of a controversial marriage or because the husband had intercourse with her and divorced her before consummating marriage, he should pay half the dowry. If the marriage is invalid according to the unanimous agreement of Muslims, as in this case, he is not entitled to pay dowry if he divorced her before consummating marriage. When a man is coerced to marry a pregnant woman out of adultery, the marriage contract is invalid according to Shafi~I and Ahmed as well as others. If he married her willingly, this is a controversial point among scholars.

Iddah (The Waiting Period)

Is a woman’s claim that she no longer menstruates accepted and she gets married according to it?

Q: A woman was divorced on the 28" of Rabi> Al-Awwal and she menstruated once until she was married to another on the 23” of Jumadah Al-Akhirah on the same year. She claimed that she menstruated three times. When the second husband knew that she menstruated only once, he divorced her on the 20" of Sha* ban on the same year. She wants to marry the second husband again and she claims that she no longer menstruates. Is her claim accepted? Is it legal to marry her?

A: A woman’s claim that she no longer menstruates is not accepted once she said so. If she said so, she is to be left for a year. If she does not menstruate during it, she could be married. If she reached an age where she no longer menstruates, she is not to be left for such year. If she no longer menstruates because of an illness or suckling a baby, she is still in her waiting period until such reason is non-existent.

This woman should have spent two ~Iddahs (waiting periods) for each of the two husbands. The second

marriage is invalid and hence it does not require that he divorce her. If she menstruated only once and blood ceased continuously, she must spend the two waiting periods after she leaves the second husband. The term of the two waiting periods is six months.

This case is applicable if she no longer menstruates. If she doubts whether she no longer menstruates or not, the waiting periods will be a year and three months. This opinion is based on some jurists’ belief that the two waiting periods do not interfere, such as Malik, Shafi” I and Ahmed. According to Abu Hanifah, they interfere but the waiting period of a woman who no longer menstruates is determined if she reached such age.
content_id:
J: Jika ia telah berhubungan intim dengannya setelah melahirkan, niat dan alasan sumpah tersebut harus dipertimbangkan. Jika ia bersumpah karena suatu alasan tertentu dan kini alasan tersebut tidak ada lagi, maka talak tidak jatuh menurut pendapat yang paling tepat yang dipegang oleh para ulama seperti Imam Ahmad dan lainnya. Jika seorang pria bersumpah

Ketika seorang pria berkata kepada istrinya: "Kamu tertalak tiga" dan ia berniat untuk menggantungkannya, apakah talak jatuh?

T: Seorang pria marah kepada istrinya dan berkata kepadanya: "Kamu tertalak tiga". Ia berkata: "sekarang", dan ia menjawab: "sekarang". Ia berniat untuk menggantungkan talak ini, apakah talak tersebut jatuh?

Jika seorang pria menceraikan istrinya sebagai candaan, maka talak tetap jatuh karena ia bermaksud menceraikannya meskipun ia tidak berniat untuk melaksanakannya. Pria yang bersangkutan tidak bermaksud menceraikan istrinya dan ia pun tidak sedang bercanda. Kasus ini seperti seorang pria yang melihat seorang wanita dan berkata "kamu tertalak" dengan keyakinan bahwa wanita itu bukan istrinya, namun ternyata itu adalah istrinya. Dalam kasus ini pun, talak tidak jatuh menurut pendapat yang paling tepat yang dipegang oleh para ulama. Wallahu a'lam.

Masalah Suraij — Apakah Masalah Suraij itu benar?

T: Apakah Masalah Suraij itu benar? Jika tidak, bagaimana dengan seorang pria yang menirunya dan bertindak sesuai dengannya? Ketika ia mengetahui bahwa hal itu tidak benar, ia bertobat dan memohon ampun kepada Allah. Apakah Allah mengampuninya?

J: Segala puji bagi Allah, Tuhan semesta alam. Masalah ini merupakan semacam bid'ah dalam Islam. Tidak satu pun dari para Sahabat Nabi, para pengikut mereka, atau keempat Imam yang mengambilnya. Sebagian ulama belakangan mengambilnya, namun sebagian besar ulama Muslim menolaknya. Jika seseorang meniru orang lain dalam masalah ini kemudian bertobat, Allah akan mengampuninya. Ia tidak boleh menceraikan istrinya meskipun ia menikahinya berdasarkan penafsiran masalah ini. Wallahu a'lam.

Apakah diperbolehkan membuat akad nikah yang di dalamnya disyaratkan talak?

T: Seorang pria menikahi seorang wanita dan wanita itu melahirkan seorang anak. Para saksi menyarankannya agar setelah ia menggauli istrinya untuk mengucapkan kepadanya: Jika aku menceraikanmu, kamu tertalak. Apakah akad ini diperbolehkan?

seorang gadis dan mengklaim bahwa gadis itu adalah anak dari suami pertama. Apakah klaim tersebut benar? Mohon diketahui bahwa ia dan suami pertama tinggal di kota yang sama dan ia tidak menuntut nafkah atau biaya untuk gadis tersebut.

J: Segala puji bagi Allah. Gadis ini tidak termasuk anak suami pertama begitu ia mengklaim demikian berdasarkan kesepakatan para ulama Muslim. Meskipun ia melahirkan gadis itu dalam keadaan dicerai dan mengklaim bahwa gadis itu adalah anaknya namun ia mengingkarinya, klaimnya tidak diterima kecuali ia dapat membuktikannya.

Menurut Abu Hanifah dan salah satu riwayat dari Ahmad, seorang wanita sudah cukup sebagai saksi dalam hal ini. Menurut Malik dan riwayat lain dari Ahmad, diperlukan dua orang wanita. Syafi'i mensyaratkan harus ada empat orang wanita. Adapun bagi pria, cukup jika ia bersumpah mengingkari bahwa anak tersebut adalah anaknya.

Jika mereka masih menikah dan hidup sebagai suami istri, terdapat dua pendapat dalam mazhab fiqh Ahmad. Pendapat pertama adalah bahwa klaimnya tidak diterima menurut pendapat Syafi'i, sedangkan pendapat kedua adalah bahwa klaimnya diterima menurut pendapat Malik. Jika masa iddahnya telah habis dan sebagian besar masa kehamilannya telah berlalu, kemudian ia mengklaim bahwa janin tersebut adalah anak dari suami pertama yang telah menceraikannya, klaimnya tidak diterima sama sekali berdasarkan kesepakatan para ulama Muslim. Jika ia mengatakan bahwa masa iddahnya telah habis dan ia melahirkan bayi setelah enam bulan kehamilan atau lebih, apakah bayi tersebut termasuk anak dari

Terdapat dua pendapat yang dipegang oleh para ulama dalam hal ini. Ahmad, Abu Hanifah, dan Ibn Suraij berpendapat bahwa anak tersebut adalah anaknya. Syafi'i dan Malik berpendapat bahwa anak tersebut bukan anaknya. Perbedaan pendapat ini berlaku jika ia tidak menikah. Namun, jika ia menikah setelah masa iddahnya habis kemudian melahirkan bayi setelah lebih dari enam bulan kehamilan, maka bayi tersebut tidak termasuk anak suami pertama berdasarkan kesepakatan para ulama Muslim.

Karena pendapat keempat mazhab fiqh telah dijelaskan, bagaimana mungkin gadis itu termasuk anak suami pertama setelah lewat enam tahun? Meskipun ia mengatakan bahwa ia melahirkan gadis itu sebelum ia diceraikan, klaimnya tidak diterima. Klaim suami pertama bahwa ia tidak melahirkan gadis itu selama masa pernikahan diterima, dan ia harus bersumpah atas hal itu. Jika ia mengatakan bahwa ia melahirkan gadis itu sebelum menikah dengan suami kedua, sementara suami pertama mengingkari hal ini, pengingkarannya diterima terutama karena klaimnya sangat terlambat hingga ia menikah dengan suami kedua. Hal ini menunjukkan bahwa ia berbohong, khususnya menurut mazhab fiqh Malik berkaitan dengan klaim-klaim yang terlambat tanpa alasan yang dapat dibenarkan.

Seorang pria menceraikan istrinya namun seorang Mufti memberikan fatwa kepadanya bahwa talak tidak jatuh. Pria itu berhubungan intim dengan istrinya dan ia melahirkan seorang anak. Apakah anak tersebut lahir dari perzinaan?

akad itu sendiri tidak sah dan istri melahirkan bayi dari hubungan intim tersebut, bayi itu termasuk anak suaminya dan mereka saling mewarisi. Bayi itu termasuk anaknya karena ia meyakini bahwa akad nikah itu sah dan talak tidak jatuh karena fatwa yang keliru atau alasan lainnya. Kasus ini juga berlaku pada akad-akad yang tidak sah berdasarkan kesepakatan para ulama Muslim. Bagaimana dengan akad yang masih diperdebatkan?

Meskipun seorang pria berhubungan intim dengan seorang wanita dalam pernikahan yang tidak sah seperti nikah mut'ah, atau pernikahan yang dilakukan tanpa wali dan saksi, namun ia meyakininya sebagai sah, anak yang lahir tetap termasuk anaknya.

Lalu bagaimana dengan pernikahan yang masih diperdebatkan meskipun telah terbukti sah menurut Al-Qur'an yang mulia, Sunnah Nabi, dan qiyas?

Ketika seorang wanita melahirkan bayi hanya dua bulan setelah akad nikah meskipun

suami belum menggaulinya, apakah pernikahan tersebut sah?

T: Seorang wanita melahirkan bayi hanya dua bulan setelah akad nikah dibuat meskipun suami belum

menggaulinya, apakah pernikahan tersebut sah? Apakah ia berkewajiban membayar nafkah?

J: Segala puji bagi Allah. Berdasarkan kesepakatan

umat Islam, bayi tersebut bukan anaknya dan

ia tidak berkewajiban membayar mahar. Namun, para ulama berpendapat dua

pendapat mengenai akad nikah:

1. Pendapat yang paling tepat adalah bahwa akad itu tidak sah menurut mazhab fiqh Malik dan Ahmad serta lainnya. Oleh karena itu, pernikahan harus diakhiri tanpa pembayaran mahar, sebagaimana berlaku dalam setiap akad yang tidak sah. Bahkan lebih baik jika seorang hakim memikul tanggung jawab mengakhiri pernikahan semacam itu sebagai upaya mencegah perselisihan.

2. Akad itu sah namun suami tidak boleh menggauli istrinya hingga ia melahirkan. Ini adalah pendapat Abu Hanifah, sedangkan Syafi'i berpendapat bahwa ia boleh menggaulinya sebelum melahirkan.

Jika ia hamil karena pernikahan yang masih diperdebatkan atau karena suami telah menggaulinya dan menceraikannya sebelum menggaulinya, ia harus membayar setengah mahar. Jika pernikahan tidak sah berdasarkan kesepakatan umat Islam, seperti dalam kasus ini, ia tidak berkewajiban membayar mahar jika ia menceraikannya sebelum menggaulinya. Ketika seorang pria dipaksa menikahi wanita yang hamil karena perzinaan, akad nikah tersebut tidak sah menurut Syafi'i dan Ahmad serta lainnya. Jika ia menikahinya dengan sukarela, hal ini merupakan perbedaan pendapat di antara para ulama.

Iddah (Masa Tunggu)

Apakah klaim seorang wanita bahwa ia tidak lagi haid diterima dan ia boleh menikah berdasarkan klaim tersebut?

T: Seorang wanita diceraikan pada tanggal 28 Rabi' Al-Awwal dan ia haid sekali hingga ia dinikahkan dengan orang lain pada tanggal 23 Jumadah Al-Akhirah pada tahun yang sama. Ia mengklaim bahwa ia telah haid tiga kali. Ketika suami kedua mengetahui bahwa ia hanya haid sekali, ia menceraikannya pada tanggal 20 Sya'ban pada tahun yang sama. Ia ingin menikah kembali dengan suami kedua dan mengklaim bahwa ia tidak lagi haid. Apakah klaimnya diterima? Apakah sah untuk menikahinya?

J: Klaim seorang wanita bahwa ia tidak lagi haid tidak diterima begitu saja ketika ia mengatakannya. Jika ia mengatakannya, ia harus ditunggu selama satu tahun. Jika ia tidak haid selama itu, ia boleh dinikahi. Jika ia telah mencapai usia di mana ia tidak lagi haid, ia tidak perlu ditunggu selama satu tahun tersebut. Jika ia tidak lagi haid karena sakit atau menyusui bayi, ia masih berada dalam masa iddahnya hingga alasan tersebut tidak ada lagi.

Wanita ini seharusnya menjalani dua 'iddah untuk masing-masing dari kedua suami. Pernikahan kedua

adalah tidak sah, sehingga tidak mensyaratkan bahwa ia menceraikannya. Jika ia hanya haid satu kali dan darah berhenti secara terus-menerus, ia wajib menjalani dua 'iddah setelah ia meninggalkan suami kedua. Masa kedua 'iddah tersebut adalah enam bulan.

Ketentuan ini berlaku jika ia tidak lagi haid. Jika ia ragu apakah ia sudah tidak haid atau belum, maka masa 'iddah adalah satu tahun tiga bulan. Pendapat ini didasarkan pada keyakinan sebagian fuqaha bahwa kedua 'iddah tidak saling bertindih, seperti pendapat Malik, Shafi'i, dan Ahmed. Menurut Abu Hanifah, keduanya saling bertindih, namun 'iddah wanita yang tidak lagi haid ditentukan jika ia telah mencapai usia tersebut.
content_ar: A: إذا he had intercourse with her بعد giving birth, his intention and the العقل for such an oath are to be taken into consideration. إذا he swore this oath: for a certain العقل and now this العقل is non-existent, no divorce is effected وفقاً لـ the most appropriate opinion held by العلماء مثل الإمام Ahmed and others. إذا a man swore عندما a man says to his الزوجة: “You are thrice divorced” and he has the intention to make it pending, is divorce effected? Q: A man got angry with his الزوجة and قال to her: “You are thrice divorced”. Sقال: “now”, and أجاب: “now”. He has the intention to make this divorce pending, is it effected? إذا a man divorces his الزوجة as a sort of joking, it is effected لأن he means to divorce her even إذا he does not intend to put it into effect. The man in question does not mean to divorce his الزوجة and he was not joking as well. This case is just like a man who saw a woman and قال “you are divorced” believing that she is not his الزوجة but it turned to be his الزوجة. In this case أيضاً, no divorce is effected وفقاً لـ the most appropriate opinion held by العلماء. الله knows best. Suraij Question Is the Suraij Question true? Q: Is Suraij Question true? إذا it is not, what about a man who imitated him and acted وبناءً على ذلك? عندما he knew that it is not true, he repented and asked الله for المغفرة. Does الله forgive him? A: All praise be to الله, the Lord of the Worlds. This question is a sort of البدعة in الإسلام. None of النبي ﷺ’s الصحابة, their followers or the four Imams adopted it. Some later العلماء adopted it, but most مسلم العلماء denied it. إذا a person imitates another as far as this question is concerned and later he repented, الله will forgive him. He should not divorce his الزوجة even إذا he married her depending on the interpretation of this question. الله knows best. Is it permissible to make a marriage contract in which divorce is stipulated? Q: A man married a woman and she gave birth to a الولد. The witnesses advised him that حين he consummates marriage with her to say to her: إذا I divorce you, you are divorced. Is this contract permissible? a girl and claimed that she belongs to the أولاً الزوج. Is such claim true? Please be acknowledged that she and the أولاً الزوج live in the same town and she did not demand for alimony or expenses for the girl. A: All praise be to الله. This girl does not belong to the أولاً الزوج حين she claimed لذا وفقاً لـ the unanimous agreement of مسلم العلماء. Even إذا she gave birth to the girl عندما she is divorced and she claimed that the girl belongs to him but he denied لذا, her claim is not accepted إلا إذا she has proved it. وفقاً لـ Abu Hanifah and one of Ahmed’s narraions, a woman is enough as a witness in this regard. وفقاً لـ مالك and the other narration of Ahmed, two women are required. الشافعي stipulates that there should be four women. أما the man, it is enough إذا he swore an oath denying that the الولد belongs to him. إذا they are still married and live as man and الزوجة, there are two opinions in Ahmed’s Juristic School. The أولاً one is that her claim is not accepted وفقاً لـ Shafi*i’s opinion, بينما the ثانياً is that it is accepted وفقاً لـ مالك’s opinion. إذا her waiting period is over and most of her pregnancy period passed, and then she claimed that such fetus belongs to the أولاً الزوج who divorced her, her claim is not accepted at all وفقاً لـ the unanimous agreement of مسلم العلماء. إذا sقال that her waiting period is over and she gave birth to a baby بعد six months of pregnancy or more, does the baby belong to the There are two opinions held by العلماء in this regard. Ahmed, Abu Hanifah, and Ibn Suraij believe that the الولد belongs to him. Shafi*i and مالك see that the الولد does not belong to him. This controversial point is applicable إذا she did not marry. Yet, إذا she married بعد her waiting period is over and then gave birth to a baby بعد more than six months of pregnancy, it does not belong to the أولاً الزوج وفقاً لـ the unanimous agreement of مسلم العلماء. As the opinions of the four Juristic Schools are clarified, how comes that the girl belongs to the أولاً الزوج بعد the lapse of six years? Even إذا sقال that she had given birth to the girl قبل he divorced her, her claim is not accepted. His claim is accepted that she does not give birth to the girl خلال the conjugal period, and he should swear an oath on that. إذا sقال that she gave birth to the girl قبل she got married to the ثانياً الزوج, بينما the أولاً denied this, his denial is accepted ولا سيما that her claim was very late حتى she married the ثانياً الزوج. This indicates that she is a liar, وبالأخص وفقاً لـ مالك’s juristic school as far as late inexcusable claims are concerned. A man divorced his الزوجة but a Mufti delivered him a الفتوى that divorce is not effected. The man had intercourse with his الزوجة and she gave birth to a الولد. Was the الولد born out of adultery? itself was invalid and the الزوجة gave birth to a baby out of such intercourse, the baby belongs to the الزوج and they inherit بعضهم بعضاً. The baby belongs to him لأن he believes that the marriage is contract is legal and that divorce is not effected لأن of a الخطأ الفتوى or any other العقل. This case is أيضاً applicable on invalid contracts وفقاً لـ the unanimous agreement of مسلم العلماء. What about the controversial contract? Even إذا a man had intercourse with a woman خلال an invalid marriage مثل that made only for pleasure (Mut*ah), or a marriage held without a custodian and witnesses, but he believes it to be legal, the born الولد belongs to him. Then what about a controversial marriage وإن كان it is proven to be legal وفقاً لـ the Holy Qur’an, النبي ﷺ’s السنة and analogy? عندما a woman gives birth to a baby just two months بعد the marriage contract وإن كان the الزوج had not consummated marriage with her, is such marriage valid? Q: A woman gave birth to a baby just two months بعد the marriage contract is made وإن كان the الزوج had not consummated marriage with her, is such marriage valid? Is he entitled to pay an alimony? A: All praise be to الله. وفقاً لـ the unanimous agreement of المسلمون the baby does not belong to him and he is not entitled to pay a dowry. Yet, العلماء hold tow opinions on the marriage contract: 1.The most appropriate opinion is that باطل وفقاً لـ the juristic schools of مالك and Ahmed as well as others. لذلك, marriage must come to an end without paying dowry, as is the case in any other invalid contract. It is rather better إذا a judge shouldered the المسؤولية of putting an end to such marriage as a sort of preventing any dispute. 2.The contract is valid but the الزوج should not consummate marriage حتى she gives birth. This is the opinion of Abu Hanifah, بينما Shafi~I believes that he can consummate marriage قبل giving birth. إذا she is pregnant out of a controversial marriage or لأن the الزوج had intercourse with her and divorced her قبل consummating marriage, he should pay half the dowry. إذا the marriage is invalid وفقاً لـ the unanimous agreement of المسلمون, as in this case, he is not entitled to pay dowry إذا he divorced her قبل consummating marriage. عندما a man is coerced to marry a pregnant woman out of adultery, the marriage contract is invalid وفقاً لـ Shafi~I and Ahmed as well as others. إذا he married her willingly, this is a controversial point among العلماء. Iddah (The Waiting Period) Is a woman’s claim that she no longer menstruates accepted and she gets married وفقاً لـ it? Q: A woman was divorced on the 28" of Rabi> Al-Awwal and she menstruated حين حتى she was married to another on the 23” of Jumadah Al-الآخرة on the same year. She claimed that she menstruated three times. عندما the ثانياً الزوج knew that she menstruated only حين, he divorced her on the 20" of Sha* ban on the same year. She wants to marry the ثانياً الزوج again and she claims that she no longer menstruates. Is her claim accepted? Is it legal to marry her? A: A woman’s claim that she no longer menstruates is not accepted حين sقال لذا. إذا sقال لذا, she is to be left for a year. إذا she does not menstruate خلال it, she could be married. إذا she reached an age where she no longer menstruates, she is not to be left for such year. إذا she no longer menstruates لأن of an المرض or suckling a baby, she is still in her waiting period حتى such العقل is non-existent. This woman should have spent two ~Iddahs (waiting periods) for each of the two husbands. The ثانياً marriage is invalid and ومن ثَم it does not require that he divorce her. إذا she menstruated only حين and blood ceased continuously, she must spend the two waiting periods بعد she leaves the ثانياً الزوج. The term of the two waiting periods is six months. This case is applicable إذا she no longer menstruates. إذا she doubts whether she no longer menstruates or not, the waiting periods will be a year and three months. This opinion is based on some jurists’ الاعتقاد that the two waiting periods do not interfere, مثل مالك, Shafi” I and Ahmed. وفقاً لـ Abu Hanifah, they interfere but the waiting period of a woman who no longer menstruates is determined إذا she reached such age.
